action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
My tummy felt so full and round,
Like a giant, puffy, sugary mound!
I ate a cake, a lollipop,
And then a whole chocolate chip!
But soon my belly started to grumble,
Like a dragon trying to get humble.
It said, "Whoa, whoa, stop right there,
You've eaten way too much sugar, my dear!"

SECTION 2
My teeth began to ache and hurt,
Like tiny hammers in my mouth, my heart!
A hole appeared right in my molar,
A sugary hole, a sticky, sweet, dark, dark, dark hole!
The dentist said, "You need a filling,
Or you'll have to have a big, big drilling!"
My gums were swollen, red, and sore,
From all the candy I had before!

SECTION 3
My head felt heavy, slow, and foggy,
Like a cloud that wouldn't let me go, foggy!
I couldn't run, I couldn't jump,
I felt like a sleepy, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky, sticky,
